name: Hydrogen
description: Lightest gas; extremely wide explosive range; clean-burning fuel

name: Helium
description: Inert noble gas; lifting gas; cryogenic coolant; non-flammable

name: Nitrogen
description: 78% of atmosphere; inert; displaces oxygen in enclosed spaces causing asphyxiation

name: Oxygen
description: 21% of atmosphere; supports combustion; essential for respiration

name: Argon
description: Third most abundant atmospheric gas; welding shield gas; inert

name: Carbon Dioxide
description: Exhaled gas; greenhouse effect; carbonation; asphyxiant at high concentration

name: Carbon Monoxide
description: Silent killer; binds hemoglobin irreversibly; incomplete combustion product

name: Methane
description: Natural gas primary component; potent greenhouse gas; swamp gas

name: Ethane
description: Found in natural gas; petrochemical feedstock

name: Propane
description: LPG fuel; easily liquefied for portable use; odorant added for safety

name: Butane
description: Lighter fuel; LPG component; refrigerant; easily liquefied

name: Acetylene
description: Welding fuel; extremely wide explosive range; unstable under pressure

name: Ammonia
description: Refrigerant; fertilizer feedstock; detectable at 5 ppm; corrosive to lungs

name: Chlorine
description: Highly toxic; water disinfectant; used as WWI chemical weapon

name: Fluorine
description: Most reactive element; toxic; reacts with almost everything including glass

name: Hydrogen Sulfide
description: Deadens smell sense at high concentration; produced by decomposition

name: Sulfur Dioxide
description: Volcanic gas; acid rain precursor; preservative (E220); respiratory irritant

name: Nitrogen Dioxide
description: Smog component; forms nitric acid with water; toxic oxidizer

name: Ozone
description: Stratospheric UV shield; ground-level pollutant; powerful oxidizer

name: Radon
description: Radioactive noble gas; seeps from soil; second leading cause of lung cancer

name: Neon
description: Noble gas; reddish-orange neon signs; fifth most abundant gas in atmosphere

name: Krypton
description: Noble gas; used in photographic flash and fluorescent lighting

name: Xenon
description: Noble gas; anesthetic properties; ion thruster propellant; headlamp gas

name: Nitrous Oxide
description: Laughing gas; dental anesthetic; whipped cream propellant; oxidizer

name: Phosgene
description: WWI chemical weapon; insidious delayed lung damage; industrial chemical

name: Hydrogen Cyanide
description: Extremely toxic; blocks cellular respiration; used in pest fumigation

name: Ethylene
description: Plant ripening hormone; most-produced organic chemical; plastic feedstock

name: Phosphine
description: Extremely toxic fumigant; pyrophoric at low concentrations; semiconductor dopant

name: Silane
description: Pyrophoric gas; semiconductor manufacturing; ignites spontaneously in air

name: Diborane
description: Extremely reactive rocket fuel; toxic; reacts with water

name: Chlorine Trifluoride
description: Hypergolic with nearly everything; more reactive than fluorine; incendiary

name: Methyl Mercaptan
description: Added to natural gas as odorant; detected at ppb levels; produced by decay

name: Hydrogen Chloride
description: Forms hydrochloric acid with moisture; corrosive; throat and lung irritant

name: Sulfur Hexafluoride
description: Extremely potent greenhouse gas; electrical insulator; deepens voice

name: Natural Gas (mixed)
description: Fossil fuel mixture mostly methane; odorant added for leak detection

name: Ethylene Oxide
description: Sterilization gas for medical equipment; carcinogenic; explosive over wide range

name: Dimethyl Ether
description: Clean aerosol propellant; potential diesel substitute; ozone-friendly

name: Formaldehyde
description: Carcinogenic preservative; building material off-gas; used in resins

name: Vinyl Chloride
description: PVC monomer; carcinogenic; was once used as aerosol propellant and anesthetic

name: Boron Trifluoride
description: Industrial catalyst; semiconductor dopant gas; forms fumes in moist air

name: Carbonyl Sulfide
description: Most abundant sulfur compound in atmosphere; grain fumigant; volcanic emission

name: Germane
description: Semiconductor manufacturing; decomposes to deposit germanium thin films

name: Nitric Oxide
description: Biological signaling molecule; vasodilator; air pollutant; quickly oxidizes to NO2

name: Nitrogen Trifluoride
description: Semiconductor etching gas; extremely potent greenhouse gas (17000x CO2)

name: Tungsten Hexafluoride
description: Densest known gas at STP; chemical vapor deposition of tungsten in chip fabrication

name: Trimethylamine
description: Cause of fishy odor in spoiled fish; amine base; used in resin production

name: Arsine
description: Extremely toxic semiconductor dopant gas; hemolytic poison; decomposes on contact with metals
